title: Will EKA2L1 be Updated to a New Version?
author: Gabrielcnnunes
body: This great amulator needs to be better, It will be updated in the near future or someday? Has it been more than two years since the last update? There are still many improvements that can be implemented.

title: [Feature Request] Add support for the N-Gage C Button and Pencil button
author: midorilandshdm
body: Hello there, I am requesting for support for adding the N-Gage C button and Pencil buttons (the one near the left and right soft keys on the original N-Gage and as both buttons on top of the keypad for QD models). Apparently EKA2L1's early versions never had a mapping for those buttons and only the regular keypad is implemented, since the C button is needed for inputting text (especially for characters or text ) on several N-Gage games like SSX Out of Bounds, Glimmerati, ONE and Requiem of Hell. Thanks.  (The original N-Gage model has the two extra buttons for the radio and music player one but are not needed for games.)

title: Slippery movement in Ashen
author: DrHingis
body: From experience, this issue doesn't occur on actual hardware. I feel it might've been a framerate issue, but even when limited to 20/30 fps, it still occurs. but occiasionally its fixed by clicking off the game, resizing the window a few times and clicking back in. This is a very strange bug and I hope when development returns it'll be fixed.

title: Lament Island crashes on Linux
author: AntonioNero
body: Lament Island runs on my Android, but it crashes on Linux. It happens when I press the button configured to the Left Softkey during the Disclaimer and Notice screen. I'm not good on showing crashes and bugs, but I hope that this message I copied from the terminal could be helpful: ``` E /home/runner/work/EKA2L1/EKA2L1/src/emu/services/src/fs/files.cpp:843 [Service.EFsrv]: Trying to open a non-existent file: E:\data\simlife\texture\ui\gokcancel.sbm while the open mode requires its availbility! E /home/runner/work/EKA2L1/EKA2L1/src/emu/services/src/fs/files.cpp:843 [Service.EFsrv]: Trying to open a non-existent file: E:\data\simlife\script\han while the open mode requires its availbility! ``` I cannot be sure that these missing files are the cause of the crash, since the game runs on Android.

title: Puyo Pop no sound

body: Puyo Pop does not play sound after the intro. I looked up people playing it on EKA2L1 on youtube and it does have sound is I'm guessing a recent update broke something.

author: brakenwagen
body: There is a game I’m trying to play Crusade of Destiny that requires you to rotate the screen. The problem is that the Touch screen mapping editor does not work when you rotate the screen and I think it because it doesn’t rotate with the screen and I can’t edit it when rotated.

body: **Describe the bug** Game crash **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Put the Call of Duty Deluxe Edition's files in e folder（"***\EKA2L1-Windows-x86_64\data\drives\e"） 2. Open callofduty in the simulator 3. Black screen and then flash back 4. The computer prompts the application terminated with code: KERN-EXEC/3 **Expected behavior** The game is running normally **Screenshots** **Your PC or smartphone specs** - Windows 11 Home Edition - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1035G1 CPU @ 1.00GHz 1.19 GHz - Intel(R) UHD Graphics (this is the first gpu) - NVIDIA GeForce MX350 (this is the second gpu) **Your emulator configuration** - Nokia N-Gage QD Dev (RH-29 - S60v1) **Log** [EKA2L1.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23834130/EKA2L1.log)

body: An emulator not launching on Windows 10. When I trying to launch it, I receiving an error: "VCRUNTIME140_1.dll, VCRUNTIME140.dll and MSVCP140.dll files not found". I have installed these files, but a new error has been received (error 0xc000007b).

body: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/94fda28a-582f-4b3a-beae-e0668f716ba8) As you can see with the screenshot and image I posted above, the dialogue box seems to be bugged on eka2l1 for android, the text is barely readable with it being displayed in cyan in contrast with the plain white background and there no portraits unlike on original hardware. Is there a fix for this?

title: Auto-dump of loaded in-RAM game files
author: lollipop-crypto
body:
Support dumping RAM game files used in game RAM during playing. KEmulator for mobile java games was able to export audio resources when the game loaded them. Useful for games that encrypt files but decrypt them when they are loaded in the RAM.

The current workaround is to dump core of the process using gcore, doesn't work well.

title: [ANDROID] 🐞 Bug Report: Crash in High Seize when gameplay starts (Audio-related)
author: c-node
body:
DESCRIPTION
High seize works all the way to actual gameplay start. The menu section and beginning cutscenes work fine, but once a match starts, the game freezes and app crashes

STEPS TO REPRODUCE
- Install and launch High Seize on EKA2L1.
- Game boots normally.
- Menu audio and intro story audio play fine without issues.
- Start a new game → after the intro, when gameplay begins (ship battle map loads).
- Emulator crashes instantly.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR
Gameplay should start normally with in-game music and sound effects enabled.

Observations
- Menu/UI sounds and intro cutscene audio work perfectly.
- Crash only occurs when the actual gameplay scene starts.
- Disabling audio in the game's settings allows gameplay to run without crashing.
- Suggests the issue is tied to streamed music / in-game audio engine rather than basic sound playback.
- Crash generates a tombstone log pointing to audio

**Your PC or smartphone specs**
- Samsung s25 ultra

**Your emulator configuration**
- N-gage & N-gage Qd (s60v1)

**Log**
I added the tombstone generated in android upon the crash, something about a mutex...

title: [ANDROID] I'm Blinded by the lights

body:
Hello team, I have came here to say if in the android version, you could have an option to make the borders black around the screen in the middle, I'm A night person and I hate just getting blinded by the white so if you could, please add that option. Thanks



- V
